name: Beverly Peer
abstract: Beverly Peer (October 7, 1912 – January 16, 1997) was an American jazz double-bassist. Peer played piano professionally early in his career before switching to bass.

works.t: You Never Know: At Long Last Love

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: It was Bill Beeson, Beverly Peer and Tommy Fulford... we do this date... all the musicians received a check... Beverly, and myself

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: Beverly Peer was a -- he's still a great -- he's still a known bass player around today. He's a great bass player.

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Beverly came with us, and he remained with us until Joe was well enough to come back.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Tate
about_quotes.evidence: I replaced a guy named Beverly Peer, a man that had been with the Chick Webb band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Tate interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
